env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with Docker + NVIDIA container toolkit + `NGC_CLI_API_KEY`. For `remote-*` modes, the corresponding `LLM_REMOTE_URL`/`LLM_REMOTE_MODEL` and/or `VLM_REMOTE_URL`/`VLM_REMOTE_MODEL` must also be set. The `/deploy` skill owns the deploy mechanic; the `/knowledge-retrieval` skill governs how retrievers are wired into a profile. This spec exercises the customization path end-to-end: the agent must apply the three patches from the `/knowledge-retrieval` skill with non-default values (custom YAML block name + non-default `default_doc_type`), then deploy and verify the custom retriever registered with the chosen settings. Self-contained — no external RAG server needed; the lvs stack bundles Elasticsearch.

expects.query: Apply the customization procedure from the `/knowledge-retrieval` skill to the lvs profile: register a second `_type: knowledge_retrieval` block named `custom_caption_retriever` with `backend: es_caption` and `backend_config.default_doc_type: aggregated_summary` (a non-default value). Add it to `workflow.tool_names` and add a one-line routing rule. Save the result to a new YAML file in the profile's `configs/` directory and point `VSS_AGENT_CONFIG_FILE` at it. Then deploy the VSS **lvs** profile on {{platform}} in {{mode}} mode end-to-end using the `/deploy` skill, autonomously.
checks: `curl -sf --max-time 15 http://localhost:8000/docs` returns exit 0 (Agent REST API responsive)
`docker logs vss-agent 2>&1 | grep -q 'knowledge_retrieval ready: backend=es_caption'` returns exit 0 (custom retriever block registered)

`docker logs vss-agent 2>&1 | grep -q 'es_caption initialised:.*default_doc_type=aggregated_summary'` returns exit 0 (custom `default_doc_type` flowed through to the adapter, proving the customization took effect)
`curl -sf --max-time 15 http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health` returns exit 0 (Elasticsearch reachable — the adapter's dependency)
